hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When both an exact registration and a wildcard match, the exact registration takes precedence and its routing logic is executed instead of the wildcard's.
The status code is 413 and the response body text is exactly 'Request too large'.

context_pointers.actual_meaning: This refers to the dynamic serialization of the backend's response body (which varies by entity type), which must be retrieved via the service's data export mechanism.

context_pointers.vague_reference: verify failure reason is not optional
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This refers to a mandatory error string that must be constructed dynamically based on the missing wildcard pattern, rather than using a cached enum value.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_request_id.json.description: A middleware ensures every request carries a request-identifier header before reaching the handler. When the incoming request already carries an identifier it is preserved unchanged. When the incoming request has no identifier a fresh one is generated and attached. The request always proceeds to the handler. Each case reports the response status and either the preserved identifier or whether an identifier was generated together with its length.

feature11_request_buffering.json.description: A middleware buffers the request body in memory up to a memory threshold, spilling to backing storage beyond it, and enforces an overall maximum body size. A request whose body fits within the overall maximum is passed to the handler, which returns its response. A request whose body exceeds the overall maximum is rejected with a payload-too-large response and the handler is not invoked. Each case reports the response status and body.

feature12_response_buffering.json.description: A middleware buffers the handler's response up to a configured maximum before forwarding it to the client. A response within the limit is forwarded with its status and body intact. A response whose content type marks it as an event stream switches to unbuffered pass-through once the handler writes its header, so its content is streamed regardless of the buffer limit. A redirect produced by the handler is forwarded with its redirect status and link body. A buffered response that exceeds the maximum is replaced with an internal-server-error response. Each case reports the response status and body.

feature13_error_pages.json.description: A middleware renders a custom error page when a downstream handler signals an error status. Error page templates are HTML files named after the status code; when a template for the signalled status exists it is rendered with the supplied arguments, otherwise a generic page is produced by the outermost middleware. Middlewares may be nested so an inner set of templates overrides an outer one for statuses it defines, while statuses it does not define fall through to the outer set. A template source that contains no usable templates is rejected with an unable-to-load error. Each case reports the response status, content type and rendered body, or the normalized load error.

cases.expected_output:
status=404 content_type=text/html; charset=utf-8 body=

custom not found

feature1_host_routing.json.description: An incoming request is routed to a backend by matching the request host against the set of services registered in the router. Each service is registered under one or more exact hostnames. When the incoming host matches a registered service the request is forwarded to that service's backend and the backend's response status and body are returned to the client. When the incoming host matches no registered service the router returns a not-found response. Each registration is reported as succeeding, and each probe reports the resulting response status together with the backend-provided body.

cases.expected_output:
deploy s1 ok
status=200 target=primary-app
status=404 target=Not Found

feature2_wildcard_routing.json.description: A service may be registered under a wildcard host pattern that matches any single-label subdomain of a base domain, or with an empty host set that makes it the catch-all for requests not matched by any other service. An exact host registration takes precedence over an overlapping wildcard registration. A wildcard pattern matches subdomains but not the bare base domain itself. Each probe reports the resulting response status and the backend-provided body.

cases.expected_output:
deploy sw ok
status=200 target=wildcard-app
status=404 target=Not Found

feature3_host_conflict.json.description: A host may be claimed by at most one service at a time. Registering a new service under a host that is already claimed by a different service is rejected with a host-already-in-use error, and the original service keeps serving that host. Re-registering an existing service by the same name under the same host is allowed and atomically swaps its backend. Each registration reports success or the normalized error category, and each probe reports the response status and backend body.

cases.expected_output:
deploy s1 ok
deploy s2 error=host_in_use
status=200 target=service-a

feature4_target_health.json.description: Before a newly registered backend begins receiving traffic it must pass a health check within a bounded deploy window. A backend whose health endpoint does not return a success status within the window causes the registration to fail with a target-unhealthy error and no routing change takes effect. A backend that passes its health check is activated and subsequently serves matching requests. Each registration reports success or the normalized error category.

feature5_tls_enforcement.json.description: When a service has TLS enabled, a plaintext request to it is answered with a permanent redirect to the same URL over the secure scheme; for methods where a redirect body is emitted the response body contains the redirect link. When a service does not have TLS enabled, a request arriving over the secure transport is rejected with a service-unavailable response. Registering a TLS-enabled service under a wildcard host is rejected because automatic certificate provisioning does not support wildcard hosts. Each probe reports the response status and body; each registration reports success or the normalized error category.

cases.expected_output: deploy s1 ok status=301 target=Moved Permanently. status=301 target=

feature6_pause_resume_stop.json.description: A service can be paused, resumed, or stopped. While paused, matching requests are held until a bounded pause window elapses and then answered with a gateway-timeout response; resuming restores normal routing. While stopped, the service answers its health endpoint with a success response but answers all other matching requests with a service-unavailable response. Each lifecycle command reports success, and each probe reports the response status and body.

cases.expected_output:
deploy s1 ok
pause s1 ok
status=504 target=Gateway Timeout
resume s1 ok
status=200 target=live-app

feature7_request_size_limit.json.description: A service may be configured to buffer request bodies with a maximum allowed size. When buffering is enabled, a request whose body fits within the configured maximum is forwarded to the backend and its response returned, while a request whose body exceeds the maximum is rejected with a payload-too-large response and never reaches the backend. Each probe reports the response status and body.

cases.expected_output:
deploy s1 ok
status=200 target=app-ok
status=413 target=Request too large

feature8_rollout_selection.json.description: A canary rollout selects a deterministic fraction of clients into a rollout group based on a per-client identifier value carried by the request. Membership is decided by a stable 32-bit FNV-1a hash of the identifier reduced modulo one hundred and compared against the configured target percentage, so the same identifier always yields the same decision and the selected fraction is reproducible across a large population. Identifiers present in an explicit allowlist are always in the group; a request that carries no identifier is never in the group. Each evaluation reports the identifier and whether it falls in the rollout group; a population sweep reports the number selected out of the total.

cases.expected_output:
value=vip group=true
value=00001 group=false
value=<none> group=false

feature9_size_limited_buffer.json.description: A size-limited buffer accumulates bytes written to it or read through it up to a configured maximum. Content that stays within the maximum is preserved and can be replayed exactly. Content that exceeds the maximum yields a size-exceeded error. A maximum of zero means unlimited. The buffer keeps a bounded amount in memory and transparently spills the remainder to backing storage, so spilled content is still preserved exactly. Each case reports the preserved content or the normalized size-exceeded error.

_constraints.input_condition: Registration contains `hosts` as a non-empty string array
_constraints.output_rule: Each registration step outputs `deploy <name> ok`

_constraints.input_condition: Registration target is not explicitly named
_constraints.output_rule: Registration fails with `error=target_unhealthy` if health check fails

_constraints.input_condition: Incoming request host matches a registered service's hostname
_constraints.output_rule: Request is forwarded to the backend; response includes status and body

_constraints.input_condition: Incoming request host matches no registered service
_constraints.output_rule: Router returns a not-found response

_constraints.input_condition: Incoming hostpartially matches a wildcard pattern matching any single-label subdomain
_constraints.output_rule: If no other exact match exists, routing succeeds with response status and body

_constraints.input_condition: Incoming host is the bare base domain (not a subdomain), no exact match found
_constraints.output_rule: Wildcard does not match; routing fails with not-found

_constraints.input_condition: Incoming host matches both an exact registration and a wildcard
_constraints.output_rule: Exact registration takes precedence (overwrites wildcard routing)

_constraints.input_condition: Registration hosts include a wildcard pattern
_constraints.output_rule: Registration fails with `error=automatic_tls_wildcard_unsupported` if TLS is enabled

_constraints.input_condition: Attempt to register a new service on a host already claimed by a different service
_constraints.output_rule: Registration fails with `error=host_in_use`; original service remains active

_constraints.input_condition: Exact re-registration of an existing service name on its own host
_constraints.output_rule: Registration succeeds (`ok`) and atomically swaps the backend

_constraints.input_condition: Service configured with TLS (`tls: true`) and incoming request uses plaintext (http)
_constraints.output_rule: Response is status 301 redirect to https; for GET request body contains link, for POST body is empty

_constraints.input_condition: Service NOT configured with TLS and incoming request uses https
_constraints.output_rule: Response is `status=400` service-unavailable

_constraints.input_condition: Service is paused and request is received
_constraints.output_rule: Request waits for `pause_timeout_ms`; after timeout returns status 504

_constraints.input_condition: Service is stopped and health endpoint is probed
_constraints.output_rule: Health endpoint returns success (200) with empty body

_constraints.input_condition: Service is stopped and non-health endpoint is probed
_constraints.output_rule: All requests return `status=400` service-unavailable

_constraints.input_condition: Request body exceeds `max_request_body_size` (buffering enabled)
_constraints.output_rule: Request rejected with status 413; body is `Request too large`

_constraints.input_condition: Request body fits within `max_request_body_size` (buffering enabled)
_constraints.output_rule: Request forwarded normally; response status and body are returned as per backend

_constraints.input_condition: Request carries no identifier header
_constraints.output_rule: Request is considered NOT in group (group=false)

_constraints.input_condition: Request identifier is in the allowlist
_constraints.output_rule: Request is always considered in group (group=true) regardless of percentage

_constraints.input_condition: Request identifier is NOT in allowlist; hash(result) < target_percentage
_constraints.output_rule: Request is considered in group (group=true)

_constraints.input_condition: Request identifier is NOT in allowlist; hash(result) >= target_percentage
_constraints.output_rule: Request is considered NOT in group (group=false)

_constraints.input_condition: Incoming request does not contain a request-identifier header
_constraints.output_rule: Middleware generates a fresh ID (36 chars) and attaches it before forwarding

_constraints.input_condition: Incoming request already contains a request-identifier header
_constraints.output_rule: Original identifier is preserved unchanged and attached

_constraints.input_condition: Response body exceeds max_bytes limit AND content-type is not treated as event stream
_constraints.output_rule: Response is replaced with status 500; body should indicate internal error

_constraints.input_condition: Response content-type indicates event stream (Server-Sent Events)
_constraints.output_rule: Buffering is skipped once header is written; stream is passed through regardless of limit

_constraints.input_condition: Response from handler is a redirect
_constraints.output_rule: Redirect status and body link are forwarded as-is, bypassing limit check

_constraints.input_condition: Status code matches a template in `outer_pages` or `root`’s `outer_pages`
_constraints.output_rule: Template is rendered with status and message arguments

_constraints.input_condition: Status code matches no available template anywhere in the chain
_constraints.output_rule: Generic error page is produced

_constraints.input_condition: Middleware chain has nested `outer` and `inner` sets
_constraints.output_rule: Inner templates take precedence for statuses they define; outer templates handle unspecified statuses

_constraints.input_condition: Template source in middleware is empty or invalid
_constraints.output_rule: Error reported as `error=unable-to-load`

hide.replacement: a specific error category or behavior flag that would be handled by a standard error strategy
